commands.help:
CPU lock check

 -r       - Show longest holder at top
 --tasks  - Show tasks in each runqueue

Helper function for reverse engineering

 --regs  - register details
 --asm <instruction> - details about an instruction

cgroup information (supports cgroup v1, v2, and hybrid)

Default (no options)
  Shows version, hierarchy list, and cgroup counts

Common analysis:
  -m          Memory usage summary per cgroup (top consumers)
  -p          Process-to-cgroup mapping
  -s <name>   Subsystem/controller overview (e.g. -s memory)

Tree views:
  -t          Compact cgroup tree (name + task count)
  -t -d       Tree with per-cgroup resource details
  -t -l       Tree with task lists

Modifiers:
  -a          Show all cgroups including those with 0 tasks
  -d          Detail mode (use with -m, -p, -s, or -t)
  -n <name>   Filter tree by cgroup name
  -c <addr>   Inspect a specific cgroup by address

Advanced:
  -g          task_group list
  -G <addr>   task_group tree
  -i / -I     mem_cgroup IDR details

scheduling information

 --classes    - Show scheduling classes
 --details    - Show details

commands.desc: Print process list in tree format
commands.help:
Print process list in tree format.

 -p          - Print process ID
 -u          - Print User ID
 -g          - Print thread count as {N}
 -s          - Print task state
 -t <pid>    - Print a specific task and its children
 -T          - Show threads as children with {name} notation
 -c          - Disable compact mode (compact identical leaf processes by default)
 -A          - Use ASCII characters for tree (default: Unicode)
 -l          - Print state color legend

Module related information.

--disasm <module name>    - Disassemble functions in a module
--details <module name>   - Show details

ipmi related information.

--smi_list      - Show smi_info list
--details       - Show additional information

commands.desc: Run insights with the currently available data

commands.help: Shows UN(D) state processes with details
Show caseno when runs by retrace server
Using AI model to check the command output

commands.desc: Execute git log/show on remote source server
commands.help:
Execute git log/show commands in remote kernel source repositories

Usage:
  git <log|show> [git-options]

Subcommands:
  log              Search git log (supports all git log options, default: --max-count=1)
  show             Show commit details (supports all git show options)

Options:
  --repos=<repos>  Comma-separated list of repos (e.g., rhel9,linux,upstream)
  --timeout=<n>    Request timeout in seconds (default: 3600)
  --verbose        Show verbose output
  -h, --help       Show help

Examples:
  git log -Sclip_push --max-count=5
  git show abc123def --stat
  git log -Sinit_new_ldt --repos=rhel9,upstream
